quizQuestion: Which mangaka created Dragon Ball?
explanation: Akira Toriyama (1955–2024) created Dragon Ball, which was serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from 1984 to 1995. The series became one of the best-selling manga franchises of all time, with over 260 million copies sold worldwide. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which manga holds the Guinness World Record as the best-selling manga series of all time, with over 500 million copies sold?
explanation: One Piece by Eiichiro Oda has sold over 500 million copies worldwide, earning a Guinness World Record as the best-selling manga series ever. It has been serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump since 1997. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which mangaka created Naruto?
explanation: Masashi Kishimoto created Naruto, serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from 1999 to 2014 across 700 chapters and 72 volumes. Over 250 million copies of the manga have been sold worldwide. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: In what year did Dragon Ball Z first air in Japan?
explanation: Dragon Ball Z premiered in Japan in 1989 as a direct continuation of the original Dragon Ball anime. It ran until 1996 and became one of the most influential anime series in Western markets. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which mangaka created One Piece?
explanation: Eiichiro Oda created One Piece, which has been serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump since 1997. The series has broken multiple sales records and Oda is one of the best-selling fiction authors of all time. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which 2020 anime film earned $504 million and became the highest-grossing Japanese film ever at the time of its release?
explanation: Demon Slayer: Mugen Train (2020), produced by ufotable, grossed approximately $504 million worldwide and surpassed Spirited Away to become the highest-grossing Japanese film and highest-grossing anime film in history. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Who is the protagonist of the Naruto manga and anime series?
explanation: Naruto Uzumaki is the title protagonist of Masashi Kishimoto's Naruto series. A young ninja who dreams of becoming Hokage, he is best known for his signature move the Shadow Clone Jutsu (Kage Bunshin no Jutsu). Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: In what year did One Piece begin its serialization in Weekly Shonen Jump?
explanation: One Piece began serialization in Weekly Shonen Jump on July 22, 1997, and continues to this day, making it one of the longest-running manga series ever. Creator Eiichiro Oda has maintained the series for nearly three decades. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which anime studio produced both Fullmetal Alchemist (2003) and Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood (2009)?
explanation: Studio Bones produced both anime adaptations of Hiromu Arakawa's Fullmetal Alchemist. Brotherhood (2009, 64 episodes) is considered the definitive adaptation as it faithfully follows the manga's story. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which mangaka created Bleach?
explanation: Tite Kubo created Bleach, which ran in Weekly Shonen Jump from 2001 to 2016 across 686 chapters. The series is part of the 'Big Three' — the trio of dominant WSJ series alongside Naruto and One Piece. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: How many episodes did Naruto Shippuden run for?
explanation: Naruto Shippuden ran for 500 episodes, airing from 2007 to 2017. It was produced by Studio Pierrot and adapted the second half of Masashi Kishimoto's manga, covering Naruto's teenage years and the Fourth Shinobi World War. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio has produced the Dragon Ball anime franchise since 1986?
explanation: Toei Animation has produced every main Dragon Ball anime series since 1986, including Dragon Ball, Dragon Ball Z, Dragon Ball Super, and Dragon Ball GT. The studio is one of Japan's oldest and most prolific animation houses. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Who is the protagonist of My Hero Academia, also known by his hero name 'Deku'?
explanation: Izuku Midoriya, nicknamed Deku, is the protagonist of Kohei Horikoshi's My Hero Academia. Born without a Quirk in a world where 80% of people have superpowers, he inherits the legendary ability One For All from the Symbol of Peace, All Might. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which mangaka created Fullmetal Alchemist?
explanation: Hiromu Arakawa created Fullmetal Alchemist, serialized in Monthly Shonen Gangan from 2001 to 2010. Arakawa is one of the most celebrated female mangaka in the shonen genre, which is traditionally male-dominated. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Along with Naruto and One Piece, which series completed the 'Big Three' dominant Weekly Shonen Jump titles of the 2000s?
explanation: Bleach, Naruto, and One Piece were collectively known as the 'Big Three' — the three simultaneously running Weekly Shonen Jump titles that dominated global anime and manga popularity throughout the 2000s. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which mangaka created Hunter x Hunter — and is married to Sailor Moon creator Naoko Takeuchi?
explanation: Yoshihiro Togashi, who also created Yu Yu Hakusho, wrote Hunter x Hunter and is married to Naoko Takeuchi, creator of Sailor Moon. Togashi is notorious for extended hiatuses due to health issues, making new chapters major events. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which mangaka created Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba?
explanation: Koyoharu Gotouge created Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba, serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from 2016 to 2020 across 205 chapters. The series became a cultural phenomenon, largely fueled by ufotable's highly acclaimed anime adaptation. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: How many chapters did the original Naruto manga run for, from 1999 to 2014?
explanation: The Naruto manga ran for exactly 700 chapters, serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from 1999 to 2014. Masashi Kishimoto later continued the story via Boruto: Naruto Next Generations. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Who is the protagonist of Jujutsu Kaisen, a high school student who swallows a cursed finger?
explanation: Yuji Itadori is the protagonist of Gege Akutami's Jujutsu Kaisen. After swallowing a cursed finger belonging to the King of Curses Ryomen Sukuna, he becomes Sukuna's vessel and enrolls in Jujutsu High to hunt down the remaining fingers. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio produced the Jujutsu Kaisen anime series?
explanation: MAPPA produced the Jujutsu Kaisen anime, which premiered in 2020. The studio is known for high-quality action animation and also produced Attack on Titan's final season. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio produced Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba, known for its breathtaking visual effects?
explanation: ufotable produced Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba, earning widespread praise for its fluid animation and cinematic visual effects, particularly the 'Total Concentration Breathing' battle sequences. Their work contributed significantly to the anime's record-breaking film performance. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Who is the rubber-bodied protagonist of One Piece, seeking to become King of the Pirates?
explanation: Monkey D. Luffy is the protagonist of Eiichiro Oda's One Piece. After eating a Devil Fruit, his body gained rubber-like properties. His goal is to find the legendary treasure called the One Piece and become King of the Pirates. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio produced the Naruto and Naruto Shippuden anime series?
explanation: Studio Pierrot produced both Naruto (220 episodes, 2002–2007) and Naruto Shippuden (500 episodes, 2007–2017). Pierrot is also known for producing Bleach and Tokyo Ghoul. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which manga series by Akira Toriyama was serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from 1984 to 1995, running 519 chapters?
explanation: Dragon Ball was serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from November 1984 to May 1995, spanning 519 chapters compiled into 42 tankōbon volumes. It popularized the power-up trope and transformed shonen manga globally. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which Yoshihiro Togashi series features a power system called 'Nen' and a protagonist named Gon Freecss?
explanation: Hunter x Hunter features Nen, a system of manipulating one's life energy into six types for combat and special abilities. Protagonist Gon Freecss is a young boy searching for his father, the legendary Hunter Ging Freecss. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: In what year did Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood premiere, the anime adaptation that faithfully follows the manga?
explanation: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood premiered in 2009, produced by Studio Bones. Unlike the 2003 adaptation which diverged from the unfinished manga, Brotherhood aired after the manga concluded and faithfully adapted Hiromu Arakawa's complete story. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which animation studio produces the My Hero Academia anime series?
explanation: Studio Bones has produced My Hero Academia since the series began airing in 2016. Bones is known for high-quality action animation and also produced both Fullmetal Alchemist anime adaptations. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: How many episodes did the original Bleach anime run for before its first cancellation in 2012?
explanation: The original Bleach anime ran for 366 episodes from 2004 to 2012, produced by Studio Pierrot. The series was later revived in 2022 with Bleach: Thousand-Year Blood War, which adapts the manga's final arc. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which Akira Toriyama manga franchise has sold over 260 million copies worldwide and popularized the power-up trope globally?
explanation: Dragon Ball has sold over 260 million manga copies worldwide, making it one of the best-selling manga franchises in history. Its influence on action manga and anime is immeasurable — concepts like power levels and transformation sequences trace directly to Toriyama's work. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Who is the orange-haired substitute Soul Reaper protagonist of Bleach?
explanation: Ichigo Kurosaki is the protagonist of Tite Kubo's Bleach. A teenager with unusual spiritual awareness, he gains Soul Reaper powers and uses a zanpakuto (soul-cutting sword) to defend the living world from evil spirits called Hollows. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which Kohei Horikoshi series is set in a world where 80% of the population has superpowers called 'Quirks'?
explanation: My Hero Academia is set in a world where the majority of humanity has developed superhuman abilities called Quirks. The story follows Izuku Midoriya, born without a Quirk, who inherits the power of the greatest hero and trains to become a professional hero himself. Source: Wikipedia.

quizQuestion: Which white-haired Jujutsu Kaisen teacher, who wears a blindfold, became one of the most popular anime characters of the 2020s?
explanation: Satoru Gojo is the most powerful jujutsu sorcerer in Gege Akutami's Jujutsu Kaisen universe. His charismatic personality and enormous power — including the Limitless technique and Six Eyes — made him a global fan favorite almost immediately after the anime's 2020 premiere. Source: Wikipedia.
